summary: Crystal‑clear 1983 matrix captures a legendary Burlington show with a blistering Scarlet > Fire > Estimated > Eyes sequence and soaring Morning Dew.
review: The matrix blend of audience and soundboard is praised as crisp, clear and virtually flawless aside from a few noticeable cuts.  The performance itself is hailed as one of the band’s finest of ’83 – highlighted by an iconic Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain → Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World run and a transcendent Morning Dew, with Jerry Garcia delivering his trademark magical solos.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both musical performance (standout songs, energy) and recording fidelity; minor cut issues slightly lower the technical score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp blend of audience and soundboard with superb balance; reviewers note a few significant cuts in both sources that affect the final jam but do not detract from overall fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Praised as solid from start to finish, with an energetic, well‑paced set that builds to a memorable second‑half sequence and a transformational Morning Dew.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone and solos are described as magical, angular and “smoking”; even when he missed some lyrics he ‘nailed ALL the music’.
band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer felt Phil’s bass didn’t come through enough in the mix.
